description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring alternating black and light gray stripes running parallel across its length. The stripes are evenly spaced and create a visually striking, high-contrast pattern reminiscent of urban tactile paving or decorative stonework. The sides of the block are solid black, giving the object a bold, modern appearance. The top surface appears to be made of a speckled, granite-like material, while the black stripes may be painted or inlaid stone. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. There are no visible engravings, markings, or interactive components on its surface.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with alternating black and light gray stripes on its top surface and solid black sides, resembling urban tactile paving.
description_view_0: Front view shows the horizontal striped pattern with the stripes running left to right across the block.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the stripes running vertically from front to back, emphasizing the block's depth.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with horizontal stripes across the surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows vertical stripes, similar to view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or pedestrian crossing zone
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
